lines.english: Lesson 49
lines.chinese: 第49课
lines.raw: Lesson 49 | 第49课

lines.english: The ideal servant
lines.chinese: 理想的仆人
lines.raw: The ideal servant | 理想的仆人

lines.english: What was Bessie's 'little weakness' ?
lines.chinese: 贝茜的“小缺点”是什么？
lines.raw: What was Bessie's 'little weakness' ? | 贝茜的“小缺点”是什么？

lines.english: It is a good thing my aunt Harriet died years ago.
lines.chinese: 幸好我姑妈哈里特在多年前就去世了。
lines.raw: It is a good thing my aunt Harriet died years ago. | 幸好我姑妈哈里特在多年前就去世了。

lines.english: If she were alive today she would not be able to air her views on her favourite topic of conversation: domestic servants.
lines.chinese: 如果她今天还活着，她就不能再对她最喜欢的话题——佣人——发表意见了。
lines.raw: If she were alive today she would not be able to air her views on her favourite topic of conversation: domestic servants. | 如果她今天还活着，她就不能再对她最喜欢的话题——佣人——发表意见了。

lines.english: Aunt Harriet lived in that leisurely age when servants were employed to do housework.
lines.chinese: 哈里特姑妈生活在那个悠闲的时代，那时人们雇佣仆人来做家务。
lines.raw: Aunt Harriet lived in that leisurely age when servants were employed to do housework. | 哈里特姑妈生活在那个悠闲的时代，那时人们雇佣仆人来做家务。

lines.english: She had a huge, rambling country house called 'The Gables'.
lines.chinese: 她有一幢名叫“山墙”的巨大而凌乱的乡村别墅。
lines.raw: She had a huge, rambling country house called 'The Gables'. | 她有一幢名叫“山墙”的巨大而凌乱的乡村别墅。

lines.english: She was sentimentally attached to this house,
lines.chinese: 她对这所房子感情深厚，
lines.raw: She was sentimentally attached to this house, | 她对这所房子感情深厚，

lines.english: for even though it was far too big for her needs, she persisted in living there long after her husband's death.
lines.chinese: 因为尽管它对她来说太大了，但在她丈夫去世后很久，她仍然坚持住在那里。
lines.raw: for even though it was far too big for her needs, she persisted in living there long after her husband's death. | 因为尽管它对她来说太大了，但在她丈夫去世后很久，她仍然坚持住在那里。

lines.english: Before she grew old, aunt Harriet used to entertain lavishly.
lines.chinese: 在她变老之前，哈里特姑妈经常大摆宴席。
lines.raw: Before she grew old, aunt Harriet used to entertain lavishly. | 在她变老之前，哈里特姑妈经常大摆宴席。

lines.english: I often visited The Gables when I was a boy.
lines.chinese: 我小时候经常去“山墙”探望。
lines.raw: I often visited The Gables when I was a boy. | 我小时候经常去“山墙”探望。

lines.english: No matter how many guests were present, the great house was always immaculate.
lines.chinese: 无论有多少客人，这幢大房子总是 immaculate（一尘不染的）。
lines.raw: No matter how many guests were present, the great house was always immaculate. | 无论有多少客人，这幢大房子总是 immaculate（一尘不染的）。

lines.english: The parquet floors shone like mirrors;
lines.chinese: 镶木地板擦得像镜子一样亮；
lines.raw: The parquet floors shone like mirrors; | 镶木地板擦得像镜子一样亮；

lines.english: highly polished silver was displayed in gleaming glass cabinets;
lines.chinese: 擦得锃亮的银器摆放在闪闪发光的玻璃橱柜里；
lines.raw: highly polished silver was displayed in gleaming glass cabinets; | 擦得锃亮的银器摆放在闪闪发光的玻璃橱柜里；

lines.english: even my uncle's huge collection of books was kept miraculously free from dust.
lines.chinese: 甚至我叔叔那一大批藏书也奇迹般地没有沾上一点灰尘。
lines.raw: even my uncle's huge collection of books was kept miraculously free from dust. | 甚至我叔叔那一大批藏书也奇迹般地没有沾上一点灰尘。

lines.english: Aunt Harriet presided over an invisible army of servants that continuously scrubbed, cleaned, and polished.
lines.chinese: 哈里特姑妈统管着一支无形的仆人队伍，他们不停地擦洗、清洁、打磨。
lines.raw: Aunt Harriet presided over an invisible army of servants that continuously scrubbed, cleaned, and polished. | 哈里特姑妈统管着一支无形的仆人队伍，他们不停地擦洗、清洁、打磨。

lines.english: She always referred to them as 'the shifting population',
lines.chinese: 她总是称他们为“流动人口”，
lines.raw: She always referred to them as 'the shifting population', | 她总是称他们为“流动人口”，

lines.english: for they came and went with such frequency that I never even got a chance to learn their names.
lines.chinese: 因为他们来去匆匆，我甚至都没机会记住他们的名字。
lines.raw: for they came and went with such frequency that I never even got a chance to learn their names. | 因为他们来去匆匆，我甚至都没机会记住他们的名字。

lines.english: Though my aunt pursued what was, in those days an enlightened policy,
lines.chinese: 尽管我姑妈在那时实行着一项开明的政策，
lines.raw: Though my aunt pursued what was, in those days an enlightened policy, | 尽管我姑妈在那时实行着一项开明的政策，

lines.english: in that she never allowed her domestic staff to work more than eight hours a day, she was extremely difficult to please.
lines.chinese: 即她从不允许她的家政人员每天工作超过八小时，但她还是非常难以取悦。
lines.raw: in that she never allowed her domestic staff to work more than eight hours a day, she was extremely difficult to please. | 即她从不允许她的家政人员每天工作超过八小时，但她还是非常难以取悦。

lines.english: While she always criticized the fickleness of human nature,
lines.chinese: 虽然她总是批评人性的反复无常，
lines.raw: While she always criticized the fickleness of human nature, | 虽然她总是批评人性的反复无常，

lines.english: she carried on an unrelenting search for the ideal servant to the end of her days,
lines.chinese: 但她毕生都在不懈地寻找理想的仆人，
lines.raw: she carried on an unrelenting search for the ideal servant to the end of her days, | 但她毕生都在不懈地寻找理想的仆人，

lines.english: even after she had been sadly disillusioned by Bessie.
lines.chinese: 即使在她被贝茜伤透了心之后也是如此。
lines.raw: even after she had been sadly disillusioned by Bessie. | 即使在她被贝茜伤透了心之后也是如此。

lines.english: Bessie worked for aunt Harriet for three years.
lines.chinese: 贝茜为哈里特姑妈工作了三年。
lines.raw: Bessie worked for aunt Harriet for three years. | 贝茜为哈里特姑妈工作了三年。

lines.english: During that time she so gained my aunt's confidence, that she was put in charge of the domestic staff.
lines.chinese: 在此期间，她深得姑妈的信任，被任命为家政人员的负责人。
lines.raw: During that time she so gained my aunt's confidence, that she was put in charge of the domestic staff. | 在此期间，她深得姑妈的信任，被任命为家政人员的负责人。

lines.english: Aunt Harriet could not find words to praise Bessie's industriousness and efficiency.
lines.chinese: 哈里特姑妈对贝茜的勤劳和效率赞不绝口。
lines.raw: Aunt Harriet could not find words to praise Bessie's industriousness and efficiency. | 哈里特姑妈对贝茜的勤劳和效率赞不绝口。

lines.english: In addition to all her other qualifications, Bessie was an expert cook.
lines.chinese: 除了她所有的其他优点，贝茜还是一位烹饪专家。
lines.raw: In addition to all her other qualifications, Bessie was an expert cook. | 除了她所有的其他优点，贝茜还是一位烹饪专家。

lines.english: She acted the role of the perfect servant for three years before Aunt Harriet discovered her 'little weakness'.
lines.chinese: 她扮演了三年完美仆人的角色，直到哈里特姑妈发现了她的“小缺点”。
lines.raw: She acted the role of the perfect servant for three years before Aunt Harriet discovered her 'little weakness'. | 她扮演了三年完美仆人的角色，直到哈里特姑妈发现了她的“小缺点”。

lines.english: After being absent from The Gables for a week,
lines.chinese: 在离开“山墙”一周后，
lines.raw: After being absent from The Gables for a week, | 在离开“山墙”一周后，

lines.english: my aunt unexpectedly returned one afternoon with a party of guests and instructed Bessie to prepare dinner.
lines.chinese: 姑妈在一个下午 unexpectedly（出乎意料地）带着一群客人回来了，并吩咐贝茜准备晚餐。
lines.raw: my aunt unexpectedly returned one afternoon with a party of guests and instructed Bessie to prepare dinner. | 姑妈在一个下午 unexpectedly（出乎意料地）带着一群客人回来了，并吩咐贝茜准备晚餐。

lines.english: Not only was the meal well below the usual standard, but Bessie seemed unable to walk steadily.
lines.chinese: 晚餐不仅远低于平时的水准，而且贝茜似乎无法稳稳地走路。
lines.raw: Not only was the meal well below the usual standard, but Bessie seemed unable to walk steadily. | 晚餐不仅远低于平时的水准，而且贝茜似乎无法稳稳地走路。

lines.english: She bumped into the furniture and kept mumbling about the guests.
lines.chinese: 她撞到了家具，嘴里不停地咕哝着关于客人的话。
lines.raw: She bumped into the furniture and kept mumbling about the guests. | 她撞到了家具，嘴里不停地咕哝着关于客人的话。

lines.english: When she came in with the last course--a huge pudding--she tripped on the carpet
lines.chinese: 当她端着最后一道菜——一个巨大的布丁——进来时，她在地毯上绊了一跤，
lines.raw: When she came in with the last course--a huge pudding--she tripped on the carpet | 当她端着最后一道菜——一个巨大的布丁——进来时，她在地毯上绊了一跤，

lines.english: and the pudding went flying through the air, narrowly missed my aunt, and crashed on the dining table with considerable force.
lines.chinese: 布丁飞了起来，险些砸到我姑妈，然后重重地摔在了餐桌上。
lines.raw: and the pudding went flying through the air, narrowly missed my aunt, and crashed on the dining table with considerable force. | 布丁飞了起来，险些砸到我姑妈，然后重重地摔在了餐桌上。

lines.english: Though this caused great mirth among the guests, Aunt Harriet was horrified.
lines.chinese: 尽管这让客人们大笑，但哈里特姑妈却吓坏了。
lines.raw: Though this caused great mirth among the guests, Aunt Harriet was horrified. | 尽管这让客人们大笑，但哈里特姑妈却吓坏了。

lines.english: She reluctantly came to the conclusion that Bessie was drunk.
lines.chinese: 她 reluctantly（不情愿地）得出结论：贝茜喝醉了。
lines.raw: She reluctantly came to the conclusion that Bessie was drunk. | 她 reluctantly（不情愿地）得出结论：贝茜喝醉了。

lines.english: The guests had, of course, realized this from the moment Bessie opened the door for them and, long before the final catastrophe,
lines.chinese: 客人们当然从贝茜为他们开门的那一刻起就意识到了这一点，而且早在最后一场灾难发生之前，
lines.raw: The guests had, of course, realized this from the moment Bessie opened the door for them and, long before the final catastrophe, | 客人们当然从贝茜为他们开门的那一刻起就意识到了这一点，而且早在最后一场灾难发生之前，

lines.english: had had a difficult time trying to conceal their amusement.
lines.chinese: 他们就一直在努力掩饰自己的 amused（好笑）。
lines.raw: had had a difficult time trying to conceal their amusement. | 他们就一直在努力掩饰自己的 amused（好笑）。

lines.english: The poor girl was dismissed instantly.
lines.chinese: 这个可怜的女孩被立刻解雇了。
lines.raw: The poor girl was dismissed instantly. | 这个可怜的女孩被立刻解雇了。

lines.english: After her departure, Aunt Harriet discovered that there were piles of empty wine bottles of all shapes and sizes neatly stacked in what had once been Bessie's wardrobe.
lines.chinese: 在她离开后，哈里特姑妈发现，曾是贝茜衣柜的地方，整齐地堆放着各种形状和大小的空酒瓶。
lines.raw: After her departure, Aunt Harriet discovered that there were piles of empty wine bottles of all shapes and sizes neatly stacked in what had once been Bessie's wardrobe. | 在她离开后，哈里特姑妈发现，曾是贝茜衣柜的地方，整齐地堆放着各种形状和大小的空酒瓶。

lines.english: They had mysteriously found their way there from the wine cellar!
lines.chinese: 它们神秘地从酒窖里“溜”到了那里！
lines.raw: They had mysteriously found their way there from the wine cellar! | 它们神秘地从酒窖里“溜”到了那里！
